FORM 10-Q TABLE OF CONTENTS: Item 1. Financial Statements 3 | Item 2. MD&A 35 | Item 1A. Risk Factors 55 | Item 8. Financial Statements 80

Item 1.    Financial Statements

Research and development: 7,677 vs 7,307

Operating income: 25,465 vs 25,619

Net income: $ 19,713 vs $ 20,123

Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements (Unaudited)

Note 1 — Summary of Significant Accounting Policies. These condensed consolidated financial statements should be read in conjunction with the consolidated financial statements and the notes thereto contained in our Annual Report on Form 10-K.

Note 2 — Revenue Recognition. The Company recognises revenue when control of the promised goods or services is transferred to the customer, in an amount that reflects the consideration expected in exchange for goods or services.

Item 2.    Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue increased 6% year-over-year to $89.5 billion for the quarter.

We expect continued growth in our Services segment going forward driven by strong subscriber momentum across Apple TV+, Apple Music, iCloud+, and Apple One.

We anticipate headwinds from foreign exchange rates in the coming quarter which will impact our reported results.

Gross margin expanded to 46.2% reflecting favorable product mix and operational efficiency gains achieved through our ongoing supply chain optimisation initiatives.

We project mid-single-digit growth in iPhone revenue for the next fiscal year based on the strong pipeline of products and services we have planned for release.

Mac revenue increased 4% year-over-year reflecting continued strength in the professional and enterprise segments driven by our M-series chip performance advantages.

Services revenue reached an all-time high of $26.3 billion, growing 14% year-over-year and reflecting the strength and breadth of our growing ecosystem.

iPad revenue decreased 5% year-over-year but we expect the upcoming product refresh cycle to drive renewed momentum in fiscal 2025 and beyond.

Wearables, Home and Accessories revenue declined 2% year-over-year reflecting challenging market conditions but we remain bullish on the long-term opportunity in this category.

Operating cash flow of $29.1 billion for the quarter reflects our strong earnings generation capability and disciplined capital allocation framework.

We returned $29 billion to shareholders during the quarter through dividends and share repurchases as part of our ongoing capital return program.

Item 1A.    Risk Factors

Our business faces significant competition in all markets where we operate. We compete with a broad range of companies that offer competing products and services in the mobile, personal computer, tablet, wearable, and streaming media player markets.

The markets for our products and services are highly competitive and subject to rapid technological change. We believe that we have competitive strengths that position us to capitalise on the opportunities that lie ahead.

Macroeconomic conditions including inflation and interest rate changes may adversely affect consumer demand. A deterioration in macroeconomic conditions, including any recession, increased unemployment levels, or reduced consumer confidence, could affect demand for our products and services.

Our supply chain relies on third-party manufacturers located primarily in Asia. We depend on the performance and reliability of our suppliers, manufacturers and logistical service providers. Events outside of our control could prevent delivery of components in a timely manner and our business and financial condition could be harmed as a result.

Our operations and financial performance depend significantly on global and regional economic conditions.

We are subject to complex and changing laws and regulations worldwide relating to privacy, data protection, consumer protection, content moderation, and competition.

Our intellectual property is subject to significant risks. Competitors have imitated and may continue to imitate our products and services. We rely on a combination of patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other intellectual property laws to establish and protect our proprietary rights.

Cybersecurity threats and incidents could adversely affect our business and reputation. Security breaches, computer malware, and computer hacking attacks have affected our products, services, and internal systems in the past.

We are exposed to significant risks associated with our international operations. Economic and political uncertainty in international markets could have a material impact on our business and financial condition.

Our financial condition and results of operations depend significantly on the performance of our products. If we are unable to develop and introduce new products in a timely manner, our financial condition may be materially adversely affected.
